channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Kalokalo and Cristo clash at Windsor
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: We have a busy day’s racing on Monday with action from Lingfield, Newton Abbot and Windsor, live on Sky Sports Racing...

item.title: Fery ready for 'attention' like Raducanu after Wimbledon run
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Arthur Fery admits he might be plunged into the media spotlight in a similar manner to Emma Raducanu following his fairy-tale run at Wimbledon.

item.title: The Open storylines: Scheffler, McIlroy, DeChambeau, Fleetwood and more!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: It has become standard procedure in recent seasons for Scottie Scheffler and Rory McIlroy to be the pre-tournament favourites at a major, but both the world’s top two head into The Open with questions about their games.

item.title: Police confirm 8 killed in terrorist attack on Benue community
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Benue Police Commissioner said he spent most of Sunday in the affected community alongside other security personnel to assess the situation and restore calm following the violence and agitations that trailed the killing of the eight people.

item.title: Tor Tiv Emerges Chairman Of Northern Christian Traditional Rulers
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Tor Tiv, His Royal Majesty (HRM) Begha U Tiv, Orchivirigh, Prof. James Ortese Iorzua Ayatse, has emerged as the chairman of the Northern Christian Traditional Rulers Forum. He called for unity, peace, justice and collective action to build a secure and prosperous Nigeria. Ayatse was elected at t

item.title: Actor Sam Neill, star of 'Jurassic Park', dies at 78
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: New ​Zealand ​actor ​Sam ‌Neill has ⁠died, ‌his ⁠family said ​in ‌a ‌statement ​on Monday.

item.title: Ex-Grand Kadi Seeks Expansion Of Sharia Court’s Jurisdiction
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A former Grand Khadi of the Kwara State Sharia Court of Appeal, Hon. Justice Solihu Muhammad (rtd ) has called on the federal government to amend the 1999 Constitution to expand the jurisdiction of Shari’a Court of Appeal beyond Islamic personal law matters. He said the current limited jurisdi

item.title: Why NIN doesn’t always lead to kidnappers — NIMC DG
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Director-General and Chief Executive Officer of the National Identity Management Commission (NIMC), Abisoye Coker-Odusote, has explained why Nigeria's National Identification Number (NIN) system does not always result in the arrest of terrorists and kidnappers. The post Why NIN doesn’t alw

item.title: 2027: Disquiet in Niger as Christians protest marginalization, make demands
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Ahead of the 2027 general election, the issue of religion has taken a new dimension in Niger State, with Christian leaders openly challenging the ruling All Progressives Congress, APC, over what they described as years of political marginalisation. Their latest demand that Governor Mohammed Umaru Ba

item.title: At least 27 killed, 22 critically injured in Bangkok bar fire
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Firefighters arrived at the scene in the Chatuchak district to find patrons running through flames to escape.

item.title: CR-SEMA Calls for Relocation of Residents From High-Risk Area After Landslide Kills 5
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: CR-SEMA Calls for Relocation of Residents From High-Risk Area After Landslide Kills 5 The Cross River State Emergency Management Agency (CR-SEMA) has called for the immediate relocation of residents living in high-risk areas following a landslide that killed five people in Ikot Anwatim

item.title: Davido opens up about his biggest fear in music
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Afrobeats star David Adeleke, popularly known as Davido, has opened up about his biggest fear. The post Davido opens up about his biggest fear in music appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Atiku, Mark, Datti Advise Students To Pursue Success Through Integrity
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Vice President and presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Atiku Abubakar, national chairman of ADC, Senator David Mark, founder and pro-chancellor of Baze University, Senator Datti Baba-Ahmed, have urged graduating students of Pacesetters Schools, Abuja, to shun shor

item.title: Hyundai Motor Begins Three-Day Strike After Wage Talks Fail
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The union and management have been in talks since May but have failed to reach an agreement, with a fifth round of negotiations collapsing on July 8. The post Hyundai Motor Begins Three-Day Strike After Wage Talks Fail appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: AWLA commends IGP Disu For Gender-Inclusive Leadership
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The African Women Lawyers Association (AWLA), Enugu State Branch, has paid a high-level courtesy visit to the State Criminal Investigation Department (SCID) to congratulate Deputy Commissioner of Police (DCP) Hope Isiani on her recent elevation to her new rank and her strategic leadership of the SCI

item.title: Super Falcons Open Casablanca Camp Ahead WAFCON 2026
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Nigeria’s Super Falcons will begin preparations for the 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations with the opening of their training camp in Casablanca on Monday (today), Completesports.com reports. Head coach Justine Madugu named a 25-player squad for Nigeria’s title defence last Friday,

item.title: 5 key lessons from the revised Kenyan Consumer Protection Act
item.dc:creator: Technext
item.description: In a significant victory for Kenyan consumers, the government recently published a revised regulation that addresses how telecom…

item.title: A trench, bases and Turkish ‘trainers’: Togo’s secret strategy against jihadists
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: For several years now, Togo has quietly been building up a plan to fight jihadist groups from the Sahel. Behind this strategy, clues point towards Turkiye. An investigation.

item.title: Rabies In Yobe: 5 People Undergo Treatment After Dog Attack
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Five residents of Malam Matari community, the outskirts of Damaturu, the Yobe State Capital, are receiving medical treatment after they were bitten by a dog confirmed to have rabies. The incident also affected livestock, prompting the Yobe State Pilot Livestock Development Programme to deploy its Ra

item.title: 🔴 Iran claims strikes against Oman, Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan. Follow our live coverage
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: 2027: Nafiu Bala’s ADC faction uploads presidential candidate, others on INEC portal
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A faction of the African Democratic Congress, ADC, led by its National Chairman,  Hon. Nafiu Bala Gombe has successfully uploaded credentials of its presidential, vice presidential and national assembly candidates on the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC portal. Nafiu, who di

item.title: CNG Hails Rescue Of Oyo Schoolchildren, Demands Action To Free Abducted Borno Pupils
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Coalition of Northern Groups (CNG) has commended the successful rescue of the schoolchildren abducted in Oyo State and demanded swift action to free abducted Borno pupils. The coalition also described the operation as proof that coordinated security efforts, effective intelligence gathering and

item.title: Why akara and kulikuli trap always succeed
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Dele Sobowale It is another election year; and suddenly, “free” money is being given at every convenient gathering. Givers, usually politically exposed individuals, call it empowerment; their paid megaphones in the media hail the altruism and, naturally expect widespread gratitude. Those of

item.title: Northern Christian Association Donates Borehole To Kurmin Wali
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Six months after bandits abducted over 170 worshippers during attacks on three churches in Kurmin Wali community, Kajuru Local Government Area of Kaduna State, the Northern Christian Association (NCA), with support from Fountain of Life Church, Lagos, has donated a borehole to the community, bringin

item.title: My Phone Was Running My Life | Then I Let TECNO’s EllaClaw Take Over
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Two days with TECNO’s EllaClaw – the agentic AI that doesn’t wait to be asked. It was a Tuesday morning, and my phone was winning. Seven unread WhatsApp messages, four of which were my mother forwarding prayer points. A calendar reminder for a meeting I had completely forgotten. Th

item.title: Oba-elect raises alarm over land dispute, appeals to Lagos CP for urgent action
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Oba-elect of Oguntedo Town in the Ori-Ade Local Council Development Area, LCDA, Satellite Town, Lagos, Modiu Oladele Olarinde, has appealed to the Lagos State Commissioner of Police, Fatai Tijani, to urgently intervene in the lingering communal crisis threatening peace and security in the commun

item.title: No Going Back On July 18 NBA Election, Committee Vows
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The chairman of the Electoral Committee of the Nigerian Bar Association (ECNBA) Chief Aham Ejelam (SAN) has said there will be no going back on the July 18 date for lawyers in Nigeria to elect their new national leaders. He vowed not to resign his appointment no matter the blackmail and threats from

item.title: APC kicks as INEC extends deadline for presidential, NASS’ nomination list
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: .Accuses electoral body of succumbing to undue pressure.Why commission moved candidates’ submission till tomorrow —IPACPDP confirms successful uploading of candidates •We don’t have any issue — LP The All Progressives Congress (APC) has rejected the decision by the Independent National Electoral Com

item.title: Ogun govt warns markets against illegal waste dumping, threatens closure of defaulters
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Ogun State Government has warned market leaders and traders to desist from indiscriminate waste disposal, saying any market found dumping refuse within its premises risks being shut down. The warning comes amid growing concerns over the accumulation of refuse and evidence of open defecation along se

item.title: Nigeria’s FX market turnover crashes 46.57% to $1.63 billion second week of July
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Nigeria's foreign exchange market recorded its sharpest weekly turnover decline of 2026, with total transactions in the FX Spot and Derivatives markets falling 46.57% to $1.631 billion in the week ended July 10, 2026, down from $3.053 billion in the preceding week. The post Nigeria’s FX market

item.title: Umahi explains how nurse associated with him died, vows legal action
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to the Minister, the deceased’s family forced open her room after becoming concerned and immediately sought medical assistance.

item.title: Police To Investigate Death Of Bafana Bafana World Cup Star Adams
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Cape Town police have opened an inquest into Jayden Adams’ sudden death after his body was found at a property in Schotschekloof on Saturday morning, MSN reports. Officials, including sport minister Gayton McKenzie, have confirmed the cause remains unknown and urged the public to avoid speculation w

item.title: Heatwaves Killed Over 2,700 In England In Two Months
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The UK and most of Europe experienced two unprecedented heatwaves in May and June, with monthly records set at 35.1°C and 37.7°C, respectively, in England. The post Heatwaves Killed Over 2,700 In England In Two Months appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Top stories from across Nigerian Newspapers, Sunday July 13
item.dc:creator: Ripples Nigeria
item.description: Here are the top stories from across the country 1. Presidency, Atiku in war of words over rescue of Oyo school children, teachers The Presidency on Saturday accused former Vice President Atiku Abubakar of failing to rejoice with the federal government several hours after the release of pupils and t

item.title: Why Peter Obi refused to debate with me – Umahi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Minister of Works, David Umahi, has stated the reason the presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, Peter Obi, declined to debate with him. Speaking through his media aide, Francis Nwaze, in a statement on Sunday, the minister said Obi knew he would not be able to defend h

item.title: Dollar to Naira exchange rate today, July 13, 2026
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Nigerian naira traded at around ₦1,382.33 to the United States dollar at the official Nigerian Foreign Exchange Market (NFEM) on Monday, July 13, 2026, according to available market data. The post Dollar to Naira exchange rate today, July 13, 2026 appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Lagos Task Force impounds 309 motorcycles, dismantles illegal structures on Lagos-Badagry Highway
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Lagos State Task Force has impounded 309 commercial motorcycles during a coordinated enforcement operation along the Lagos-Badagry Expressway as part of efforts to improve traffic flow, restore order and enhance security along the busy transport corridor. The exercise forms part of the Lagos State G

item.title: Bauchi Begins 2027 Hajj Registration, Fixes Initial Deposit At N7m
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Bauchi State Muslim Pilgrims Welfare Board has commenced registration for the 2027 Hajj and fixed an initial deposit of N7 million for intending pilgrims. The board’s executive secretary, Imam Abdurrahman Ibrahim Idris, disclosed this at a press conference held at the agency’s headquarters in

item.title: Super Eagles goalkeeper Nwabali completes Chippa United return
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Super Eagles of Nigeria goalkeeper Stanley Nwabali has rejoined South African club Chippa United after leaving the club earlier this year. The post Super Eagles goalkeeper Nwabali completes Chippa United return appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: FTSE 100 Futures, Pound Fall as Iran Tensions Ramp Up
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets

item.title: Ijebu Obas endorse Yayi, deputy for Ogun 2027
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: •Back Abiodun’s senatorial bid The Ijebu Traditional Council of Obas has unanimously endorsed the All Progressives Congress (APC) 2027 gubernatorial candidate in Ogun State, Senator Olamilekan Adeola Solomon, popularly known as Yayi, and his deputy, Alhaja Kudirat Adegunwa-Balogun. The council also

item.title: World Cup 2026: France Won’t Focus On Yamal Alone –Konate
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: New Real Madrid defender Ibrahima Konate has disclosed that France will not specifically tailor their tactical plan around Lamine Yamal ahead of their FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final clash against Spain. He dismissed suggestions that France fears the Spanish teenager, warning that focusing exclusivel

item.title: Nigeria must reform policing, not just create state police – Sam Amadi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Director, Abuja School of Social and Political Thoughts, Sam Amadi, has called on the Federal Government to reform policing and not just to create state police. Amadi made this call on Sunday when he appeared as a guest in an interview on Arise Television. He reiterated the need for greate

item.title: US-Iran Conflict Fuels 111% Six-Month Surge In Nigerian Energy Stocks
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: …Analysts Predict Fresh Rally Amid Strong Fundamentals Geopolitical tensions arising from the US-Iran conflict fuelled a remarkable 111.13 per cent rally in Nigeria’s oil and gas stocks during the first half of 2026, making the sector the best-performing segment of the Nigerian Exchange Limite

item.title: Transfer: Laporta speaks on Raphinha leaving Barcelona after Gordon, Adeyemi’s arrivals
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Barcelona president, Joan Laporta, has ruled out any possibility of Raphinha leaving the club this summer transfer window. Laporte insisted that the arrivals of Anthony Gordon and Karim Adeyemi to Barcelona don’t mean Raphinha is leaving the LaLiga champions this summer. Barcelona recently sig

item.title: Benue: Amnesty demands independent probe into killings In Otukpo Nobi
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Amnesty International has called on the Nigerian authorities to carry out an “independent, impartial and effective investigation” into the deadly attack on Otukpo Nobi and Akpachi-Ugboju communities in Benue State, in which at least 10 people were killed on Sunday. In a statement, the rights group s

item.title: Transfer: Chelsea to sign Real Madrid star for Xabi Alonso
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Chelsea are keen to sign Real Madrid defender, Alvaro Carreras for their manager, Xabi Alonso. Alonso is searching the summer transfer window market for a new left-back after Marc Cucurella’s move to Real Madrid. The former Bayer Leverkusen coach was keen for Cucurella to stay at the Premier League

item.title: Banks slash lending, cut N5.4trn across key sectors
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: …As agric, finance, others attract fresh loans lForbearance withdrawal, FX stability reshape bank lending — Experts lIt reflects structural challenges — MAN By Babajide Komolafe Deposit Money Banks (DMBs) slashed lending to oil and gas, information and communication technology (ICT) and six other ke

item.title: Nigeria’s crude oil production hits 1.56mbpd in June, highest since April 2020
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Nigeria’s crude oil production rose to an average of 1.56 million barrels per day (mbpd) in June 2026, marking the country’s highest monthly crude oil output since April 2020. The post Nigeria’s crude oil production hits 1.56mbpd in June, highest since April 2020 appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: Transfer: Paul Scholes raises question over Man Utd’s deal to sign Santos
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Manchester United legend, Paul Scholes, has raised a question over the Red Devils’ deal to sign Andrey Santos from Chelsea. The former England midfielder also suggested that the club’s fans are unlikely to be excited by the midfielder’s impending arrival at Old Trafford. Man United have

item.title: TSMC Sales Surge 36% in Fresh Sign of AI Spending Momentum
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. reported quarterly sales rose 36%, meeting high expectations and signaling global demand for AI hardware remains intact.

item.title: Sheikh Hamad: The Arab leader who broke Israel’s siege on Gaza
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: A look at Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani's enduring legacy of solidarity and support for the Palestinian people.

item.title: Rescued Oriire pupils may not return to schools, parents fear fresh attacks
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Parents of pupils abducted from schools in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State have expressed fears about returning their children to the affected schools, saying the trauma of spending 56 days in captivity has left many families unwilling to take further risks. The post Rescued Oriire pupils

item.title: Jannik Sinner retains Wimbledon title after victory over Alexander Zverev
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Jannik Sinner retains his crown. The 24-year-old Italian won the Wimbledon title for the second consecutive year after defeating Germany’s Alexander Zverev in four sets. It is Sinner’s fifth Grand Slam title, his first of the year, further strengthening his position as world No. 1.

item.title: Oriire rescue: Gbenga Hashim lauds security forces
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: …Says criminals can be defeated with strong political backing Presidential candidate of the Accord Party for the 2027 general election, read more Oriire rescue: Gbenga Hashim lauds security forces

item.title: World Cup: Konate dismisses Yamal’s warning ahead of Spain semi-final
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: France defender Ibrahima Konate has brushed aside Lamine Yamal's confident pre-match comments as Les Bleus prepare to face Spain in Tuesday's 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-final. The post World Cup: Konate dismisses Yamal’s warning ahead of Spain semi-final appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: US and Iran Trade Fresh Strikes, Dispute Whether Hormuz Is Open
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes overnight into Monday as they continued their tit-for-tat attacks while issuing conflicting declarations over whether the Strait of Hormuz was open to shipping.

item.title: Nigerians now hold $59bn in Cryptocurrency assets —FDC
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: As BTC declines 0.92% in 24 hours to $59,368 NIGERIANS have accumulated an estimated $59 billion in cryptocurrency holdings, according to data released by Financial Derivatives Company (FDC) Limited during its July breakfast session. The figure highlights the country’s emergence as one of Africa’s a

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 36
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Good morning to readers. Daily Sun brings you the major stories from Nigerian Newspapers on Monday morning. The post Nigerian Newspapers: Major stories on Monday appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: …Identified cattle traders, leasers of farmlands for grazing, told to quit business in Oghara …We can no longer fold our read more As insecurity heightens, Delta communities begin to fight back

item.title: Saudi Arabia grants six-month extension for expired work permit regularisation
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Saudi Arabia has extended the grace period for employers to regularise expired work permits for foreign workers until December 31, 2026, providing businesses with an additional six months to address outstanding compliance issues. The post Saudi Arabia grants six-month extension for expired work perm

item.title: INEC deadline: Parties seeking extension not ready for politics — Morka
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The National Publicity Secretary of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Felix Morka, has criticised political parties that sought an extension of the Independent National Electoral Commission's (INEC) deadline for submitting candidates for the 2027 general election, saying they are not prepared for

item.title: These are unprecedented times for England - enjoy them
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: A record of four semi-finals in the past five international tournaments for England is outstanding. It is time to accept that this is where England belong.

item.title: Ukrainian drone strikes kill at least 3, wound 5 in Moscow region
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Drone attacks killed at least three people and injured five others in the Moscow region, governor Andrey Vorobyov said on Telegram Monday. Ukraine has been intensifying its strikes on Russia's energy targets in response to Moscow's barrages.

item.title: South Africa Plans First Strategic Oil Boost Since Apartheid Era
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: South Africa plans to increase its strategic oil reserves for the first time since the apartheid government stockpiled crude, adding to measures across the continent to mitigate supply shocks.

item.title: Global Funds Return to Indian Stocks With $1 Billion in Buying
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Foreign investors poured more than $1 billion into Indian equities last week, with Goldman Sachs Group Inc. predicting further inflows as a stable currency and improved earnings expectations prompt funds to add to their holdings.

item.title: Banks deposit N4.15trn with CBN as excess liquidity persists
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: NIGERIA’S banking sector deposited a total of N4.15 trillion with the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) through the Standing Deposit Facility (SDF) during the week, highlighting persistent excess liquidity in the financial system, despite ongoing monetary tightening measures. Market data showed that dep

item.title: Stock market rebounds as investors gain N9.3trn in 5 days
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Peter Egwuatu The Nigerian stock market recorded a strong rebound closing bullish last week’s trading with investors gaining N9.342 trillion. The Nigerian Exchange Limited, NGX, had recorded a loss of over N1.8 trillion previous week following a wave of selloffs that had hit the market since last

item.title: How former Emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani built Qatar’s economy
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Sheikh Hamad, who passed away aged 74 on Sunday, reshaped Qatar's economy by building lasting wealth and growth.

item.title: Nigeria: Court Restricts Lawyers in Police Force but Not Appointed As Legal Officers to Only Prosecution
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Premium Times] The judge also ordered the police force, Police Service Commission (PSC) and the Inspector-General of the Federation to deploy "police officers who are legal practitioners to every Police Division in Nigeria to assist with the enforcement of human rights in policing at the Divisions.

item.title: Iran attacks US military bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Iran launches attacks on Gulf states, targeting US military assets in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan.

item.title: Beavis Ultrasound PnP ISA Sound Card Replica
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://github.com/schlae/BeavisUltrasound Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48888193 Points: 19 # Comments: 4

item.title: China’s Oil Imports May Be Set to Recover as Stockpiling Returns
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China’s crude imports look poised to recover from a months-long slump as the country relaxes fuel export curbs, raises run rates and snaps up prompt Middle East supplies, with analysts and traders forecasting a return to strategic stockpiling later this year.

item.title: FG’s exam fee hike raises dropout fears
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The federal government has been criticized for its decision to increase the registration fees for the West African Examinations Council (WAEC) and the National Examinations Council (NECO) Senior School Certificate Examinations (SSCE) to N50,000. The new fee, approved by the Federal Ministry of Educa

item.title: Kurfi and the radarada in Katsina
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: If Katsina State governor, Malam Dikko Umaru Radda, were a Yoruba man, the people would say he is living his name. I do not know what... The post Kurfi and the radarada in Katsina appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: 1 agency, 2 heads: Wike’s ally remains in office despite Tinubu’s directive
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: A leadership crisis is brewing at the Border Communities Development Agency (BCDA) as a governorship aspirant under the All Progessives Congess (APC) in Rivers State, Dr Dakorinama Alabo George, remains in office as the Executive Secretary, despite his replacement by the Presidency with a former Hou

item.title: Nigeria’s tax transition and the rule of law: Why legal certainty must remain at the heart of tax reform
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Introduction The enactment of Nigeria’s Tax Acts 2025 marks one of the most ambitious tax reform programmes in the country’s read more Nigeria’s tax transition and the rule of law: Why legal certainty must remain at the heart of tax reform

item.title: ‘He was our hope’: Father mourns 28-yr-old lieutenant killed in Oyo rescue operation
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Mr Isaac Alabura, father of Lt. Felix Ademe Isaac, the 28-year-old Nigerian Army officer who died during an operation to rescue abducted teachers and pupils in Oyo State, said his family is yet to recover from the painful loss. The young officer was killed while leading troops during the rescue oper

item.title: Parents of abducted Borno pupils mount pressure after Oyo rescue
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Families of pupils abducted from Mussa Primary and Junior Secondary School in Askira-Uba Local Government Area of Borno State have appealed to the federal government to intensify efforts to rescue their children, saying they feel abandoned two months after the incident. Their appeal comes barely 48

item.title: Elderly Palestinian describes being beaten up by Israeli settlers
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Israeli settlers assaulted 79-year-old Ibrahim al-Jabour and his family in Masafer Yatta in the Occupied West Bank.

item.title: Parents demand N100,000 minimum pay for Sokoto teachers
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Parents of students at the Federal Government College (FGC) Staff School, Sokoto, have criticised the N60,000 monthly salary paid to teachers, describing it as inadequate, especially for educators who have spent more than 20 years in service. The demand was made during the Parent-Teacher Association

item.title: Nigeria: No Info About Our 78 Children Kidnapped By Boko Haram Weeks Ago, Parents Lament
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Leadership] Parents of the 42 Mussa Primary and Junior Secondary School pupils and students, as well as the 36 Lassa Government Day Secondary School Students,in Askira-Uba local government area of Borno State, still in the hands of the Boko Haram terrorists weeks after their abduction, have gone sp

item.title: Nigeria: Benue, Plateau Communities Hit By Fresh Attacks, 27 Killed
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Vanguard] No fewer than 27 persons have been killed in separate attacks on communities in Benue and Plateau states, following fresh assaults by suspected armed groups that have left residents mourning and demanding urgent government intervention.

item.title: Nigeria: Notorious Zamfara Bandit Kingpin Kachalla Yellow Killed in Gun Duel
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Vanguard] A notorious bandit commander, identified as Kachalla Sani Yellow, has been killed in Zamfara State by local hunters and members of the Civilian Joint Task Force.

item.title: Malawi: MPs Exposed Over Alleged Plot to Extend Term of Office
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Nyasa Times] Human rights activist Redson Munlo has warned Members of Parliament against what he claims is a plan to extend their term of office from five to seven years.

item.title: East Africa: Somalia, Egypt FMs Discuss Bilateral Ties, Horn of Africa, Reject Recognition of Somaliland
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Shabelle] Mogadishu -- Somalia's Foreign Minister Abdisalam Ali Dhaay held a telephone conversation with his Egyptian counterpart, Badr Abdelatty, to discuss bilateral relations and regional developments, with a particular focus on the Horn of Africa, Somalia's foreign ministry said on Sunday.

item.title: Mozambique: Two More Mozambican Nationals Killed in Xenophobic Attacks
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [AIM] Maputo -- Two more Mozambican nationals have lost their lives following xenophobic attacks committed by South African citizens against black Africans.

item.title: Namibia: Fishrot Victims Seek Justice in Iceland
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Namibian] More than 1 000 former Namibian fisheries workers have asked Icelandic prosecutors to recognise them as victims in criminal proceedings linked to the Fishrot corruption scandal.

item.title: Zimbabwe: Teachers' Union Mobilizes Against Mnangagwa's Term Extension, Plans Parliament Protest
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [New Zimbabwe] The Amalgamated Rural Teachers Union of Zimbabwe (ARTUZ) has resolved to join a broader alliance of civic groups to resist Constitutional Amendment Act No. 3, warning that the changes threaten democratic protections.

item.title: SPPG unveils app for real-time crime reporting
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: A team from the School of Politics, Policy and Governance (SPPG) has launched Synited Safe, a digital platform designed to strengthen community-based security by enabling Nigerians to report crimes, suspicious activities, and emergencies in real-time. The Group Team Lead of Synited Safe, Uche Ekeled

item.title: US and Iran trade fire as tensions rise over Strait of Hormuz
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Within hours of fresh US attacks, Iran said it had struck US military bases in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain.

item.title: NIGERIA DAILY: ‘Nobody Has Been Forgotten, Efforts Ongoing To Rescue Borno Schoolchildren’ — Bayo Onanuga
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Download Here: When children are kidnapped, every passing day brings renewed pain and uncertainty for their families. While abducted schoolchildren in Oyo State have regained their freedom, those taken in Borno State remain in captivity more than fifty days later. NIGERIA DAILY: Delayed Candidate Li

item.title: Repentant Boko Haram Member Reveals They Use Ai To Make Bombs
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: Former Boko Haram commanders have confessed that the extremist group now uses artificial intelligence (AI) to enhance attacks in Nigeria.According to a New York Times report citing University of Cambridge researcher Antonia Juelich, insurgents used AI chatbots to learn how to modify motorcycles for

item.title: Police comb Kogi forests for abducted FRSC officials, mourners
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Police and other security agencies have launched a rescue operation to secure the release of nine mourners, including two officials of the Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC), who were abducted by suspected kidnappers along the Ochadamu-Ejule road in Ofu Local Government Area of Kogi State. Security so

item.title: Experts advocate improved investments in cancer care, research
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Health experts, researchers and policymakers have called for increased investments in cancer research, local production of cancer medicines and stronger regional collaboration to improve access to quality cancer care in Nigeria and across Africa. They made the call during a media session at the Best

item.title: State police Tinubu’s masterstroke against insecurity – Barau
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Deputy President of the Senate and Chairman of the Senate Committee on Constitution Review, Senator Barau I. Jibrin, has described President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s quest to establish State Police as a “masterstroke” aimed at decisively addressing insecurity in the country. Speaking on Radio Nigeria

item.title: Are Messi, Mbappe, Yamal and Kane in the best World Cup semifinals yet?
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: France, Spain, England and Argentina have the stars for the semifinals, but will the performances match the reputations?

item.title: The naira float: What it fixed, what it broke
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Chukwuemeka ran a printing business in Onitsha. In May 2023, he imported paper and ink from China, paying roughly N460 read more The naira float: What it fixed, what it broke
